aliases: beef cheeseburger with lettuce tomato and ShackSauce

english: beef cheeseburger with lettuce tomato and ShackSauce

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002383 covers hamburger as a food product; Shackburger is a branded cheeseburger variant with proprietary sauce — closest defensible FoodOn anchor used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKW (Hamburger) is the closest defensible code; Shackburger includes cheese and proprietary ShackSauce not captured in the base code.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No sufficiently specific SNOMED CT code identified for a branded cheeseburger product; null used to maintain ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Shackburger weighs approximately 190 g and constitutes a single-serve meal item typically consumed at lunch or dinner.

nutrient_source.title: Shackburger — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Shackburger (cr_id G00010996) with confidence 0.95. All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken from this canonical HPB record. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for cheeseburger (USDA SR Legacy #21097) as HPB does not report saturated fat separately; this adaptation is documented here.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate based on white bread bun and mixed burger composition
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Shackburger. GI of 60 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for a hamburger on a white-flour bun (GI ~61, Atkinson et al. 2021). The brioche bun used in the Shackburger has a similar refined-flour composition. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (60) × available carbohydrate per serving (29.57 g/100g × 190 g serving = 56.2 g) / 100 = 33.7 g GL.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 487 mg/100g with low potassium offset (197 mg/100g) and low dietary fibre (1.8 g/100g)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid the Shackburger due to its high sodium content (487 mg/100g); a single 190 g serving delivers approximately 925 mg sodium — nearly 40% of the 2,400 mg daily limit — and the low potassium-to-sodium ratio provides insufficient cardiovascular offset.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate (29.57 g/100g) from brioche bun with low dietary fibre (1.8 g/100g) producing estimated GI of 60 and glycemic load of 33.7 per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume the Shackburger only occasionally and in reduced portions, as the brioche bun delivers a high glycemic load (~33.7 per serving) that will cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; requesting a lettuce wrap instead of the bun substantially reduces glycemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~3.8 g/100g estimated) from beef patty and processed cheese, combined with cholesterol (29 mg/100g) and sodium (487 mg/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit Shackburger consumption to occasional treats, as the combination of saturated fat from the beef patty and American cheese, elevated sodium, and estimated trans fat (0.1 g/100g) collectively raises LDL cholesterol and blood pressure risk; requesting no cheese and reduced ShackSauce lowers saturated fat and sodium load.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Red meat arachidonic acid (omega-6 pro-inflammatory pathway), estimated trans fat 0.1 g/100g, and nightshade ingredients (tomato)
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid or minimise the Shackburger as the beef patty is rich in arachidonic acid which amplifies inflammatory pathways, the processed cheese and griddle cooking contribute estimated trans fat (0.1 g/100g), and tomato as a nightshade vegetable may trigger individual RA flares.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Shackburger is a signature cheeseburger offered by Shake Shack, a fast-casual American restaurant chain with outlets in Singapore and globally. It consists of a 100% all-natural Angus beef patty griddled to order, topped with American cheese, crisp green-leaf lettuce, ripe tomato slices, and the proprietary ShackSauce — a creamy, tangy mayonnaise-based condiment — all served on a soft, lightly toasted brioche bun. Per 100 g, the Shackburger provides 263 kcal, 13.3 g protein, 10.18 g fat, and 29.57 g carbohydrate, with 487 mg sodium and 1.8 g dietary fibre. The high sodium content (487 mg/100g) and refined-carbohydrate brioche bun make it unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension or Type 2 Diabetes without modification. The saturated fat from the beef patty and American cheese raises cardiovascular concern. A standard serving weighs approximately 190 g, delivering roughly 500 kcal, 925 mg sodium, and a glycemic load of approximately 33.7. The Shackburger is classified as a processed mixed dish in the Western cuisine category and is commonly consumed at lunch or dinner. Clinically, it is best treated as an occasional indulgence for individuals with cardiometabolic conditions, with modifications such as lettuce wrap substitution, reduced sauce, and no cheese recommended to lower glycemic and sodium burden.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Shackburger?

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry for Shackburger (cr_id G00010996) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA SR Legacy cheeseburger entry as HPB does not report it separately; this is the only adapted field. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using a comparable hamburger-on-white-bun entry.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for the Shackburger is not reported in the HPB Foodo database or USDA Branded Foods as of 2026-04-16. trans_fat_g set to 0.1 based on USDA SR Legacy cheeseburger analogue and input health_suitability notes; this is an estimate. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrients are not reported in the HPB Foodo entry for this dish and no sufficiently close USDA Branded Foods entry was available to supply them without unacceptable estimation error. saturated_fat_g of 3.8 g/100g is adapted from USDA SR Legacy entry #21097 (Cheeseburger, plain, single patty) as the closest approved-source analogue; this adaptation is documented in nutrient_source. Glycemic load per serving calculated using 190 g serving size: GL = 60 × (29.57 × 1.90) / 100 = 60 × 56.18 / 100 = 33.7. The Shackburger is a Western fast-casual dish sold in Singapore; multilingual local-name fields (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) are null because the dish is a branded English-named product with no established local-language translations in common use — the English brand name is used universally across all language communities in Singapore.
